name: Maps

values.name: defaultModel
values.comment: The default model is a map zoomed into Sydney, Australia with no markers.

values.name: update
values.type: Msg msg -> Model msg -> ( Model msg, Cmd (Msg msg) )

values.name: updateMap
values.comment:
Change the map inside of a model.

For example, set the width/height of a map and zoom into Seoul, South Korea:

    import Maps.Geo
    import Maps.Map as Map

    let
      seoul = Maps.Geo.latLng 37.532600 127.024612
    in
      model
      |> updateMap (Map.setHeight 600)
      |> updateMap (Map.setWidth 1000)
      |> updateMap (Map.viewBounds <| Maps.Geo.centeredBounds 10 seoul)

See [Maps.Map](./Maps-Map) for documentation of the Map functions.

values.name: updateMarkers
values.comment:
Change the markers inside of the model

For example, add markers for some Sydney attractions and then another marker for the city center:

    import Maps.Geo
    import Maps.Marker as Marker

    let
      attractions =
        List.map (uncurry Maps.Geo.latLng)
          [ (-33.852324, 151.210819)
          , (-33.856872, 151.215239)
          , (-33.870397, 151.208835)
          ]
      sydney = Maps.Geo.latLng -33.865143 151.209900
    in
      model
      |> updateMarkers (\markers -> List.map Marker.create attractions ++ markers)
      |> updateMarkers ((::) (Marker.create sydney))

See [Maps.Marker](./Maps-Marker) for documentation of the Marker functions.
values.type: (List (Marker msg) -> List (Marker msg)) -> Model msg -> Model msg

name: Maps.Marker

aliases.name: Marker
aliases.comment:
There are currently two types of marker:

 * A default marker
 * A custom HTML marker

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create a default style of marker at the given latitude/longitude.

    import Maps.Marker
    import Maps.Geo

    newYork = Maps.Geo.latLng 40.730610 -73.935242
    newYorkMarker = Maps.Marker.create newYork

values.name: createCustom
values.comment: Create a custom HTML marker at the given latitude/longitude.

name: Maps.Map

aliases.name: Map
aliases.comment:
The map type contains all the information necessary to display a map on the screen.
The map type is opaque, so use the functions in this module to maniuplate the map.

values.name: move
values.comment:
Move the map a given number of pixels in the x and y dimensions.

For example, up 10px and right 10px:

    map
    |> move { x = 10, y = -10 }

values.name: moveTo
values.comment:
Move the center of the map to a specific location.

For example, move the map to Shanghai:

    map
    |> moveTo (Maps.Geo.LatLng 31.267401 121.522179)

values.name: setHeight
values.comment: Set the height, as displayed on the screen, of a given Map.

values.name: setTileServer
values.comment:
Set the tile server.

The format is a URL with {x} {y} {z} placeholders for the x, y and zoom coordinates.

values.name: setTileSize
values.comment:
Set the width/height in px of a tile.
Note, this is dependent on the tile server, and the default of 256px is almost always correct.

values.name: setWidth
values.comment: Set the width, as displayed on the screen, of a given Map.

values.name: setZoom
values.comment:
Sets the zoom to a specific level

For example, zoom all the way out

    map
    |> zoom 0

Or all the way in:

    map
    |> zoom 19

values.name: viewBounds
values.comment:
Move and zoom the map to cover given the bounds.

For example, view th bounds of Madagascar:

    let
      madagascar =
        Maps.Geo.bounds
          (Map.Geo.LatLng -11.9519639 50.48377989999999)
          (Maps.Geo.LatLng -25.6065157 43.1851395)
    in
      map
      |> viewBounds madagascar

values.name: zoom
values.comment:
Zoom into the center of the map.

For example zoom out two levels:

    map
    |> zoom -2

values.name: zoomTo
values.comment:
Zoom into an x,y co-ordinate on the map.

For example, zoom into the top left corner of the map:

    map
    |> zoom 1 { x = 0, y = 0}

name: Maps.Convert

aliases.name: MapSizes
aliases.comment:
The size properties of a map.
The conversion functions that require this type can just be passed a map type.
aliases.type: { a | tileSize : Float, zoom : ZoomLevel, width : Float, height : Float, center : LatLng }

values.name: latLngToScreenOffset
values.comment:
Take latitude/longitude anc convert it to an offset from the top left of the map.
Note that it requires the dimensions of the map (or the map itself) to calculate this conversion.

values.name: screenOffsetToLatLng
values.comment:
Take an offset from the top left of the map and convert it to a latitude/longitude.
Note that it requires the dimensions of the map (or the map itself) to calculate this conversion.

name: Maps.Geo

aliases.name: Bounds
LatLng

values.name: bounds
values.comment:
Create a Bounds using a northeast and southwest point.

For example, the bounds of Ecuador

    ecuador =
      bounds
        { northEast = latLng 1.4284875 -75.188794
        , southWest = latLng -5.0143511 -81.08498089999999
        }

values.name: centeredBounds
values.comment:
Create a Bounds centered on a location with a given zoom level.

For example, zoomed into the streets of Baku, Azerbaijan:

    baku =
      centeredBounds
        14
        (latLng 40.409264 49.867092)

values.name: latLng
values.comment:
Create a LatLng.

For example:

    latLng 45 -175
